request.userPrompt: list all the student name and the courses they have enrolled

response.generatedSQL:
SELECT 
    s.first_name, 
    s.last_name, 
    c.course_id
FROM 
    Students s
JOIN 
    Enrollments e ON s.student_id = e.student_id
JOIN 
    Classes c ON e.class_id = c.class_id;
response.englishExplanation: This query retrieves the first and last names of students along with the IDs of the courses they are enrolled in. It combines information from three different tables: Students, Enrollments, and Classes, by matching student and class identifiers. There are no specific filters or conditions applied, and the results are not ordered or grouped in any particular way. The purpose of this query is likely to provide a list of students and the courses they are taking, which could be useful for administrative or reporting purposes in an educational setting.

request.userPrompt: list all the products which is heavier than 1 kg

response.generatedSQL:
SELECT p.productid, p.name, p.description, p.price, p.stockqty, p.sku
FROM products p
JOIN productattributevalues pav ON p.productid = pav.productid
JOIN attributes a ON pav.attributeid = a.attributeid
WHERE a.name ILIKE 'Weight' AND CAST(pav.valuedecimal AS numeric) > 1 AND a.unit ILIKE 'kg';
response.englishExplanation: The query retrieves information about products, including their ID, name, description, price, stock quantity, and SKU. It specifically looks for products that have a weight attribute greater than 1 kilogram. The data is filtered to only include products with this weight characteristic. This query is likely used to identify and analyze products that are heavier than 1 kg, which could be useful for inventory management, shipping considerations, or marketing strategies focused on heavier products.
